name: changelog
description: Maintains IdeaVim changelog (CHANGES.md) and build.gradle.kts changeNotes. Use when updating changelog, documenting releases, or reviewing commits/PRs for changelog entries.
capabilities: Analyzes git commits and PRs to identify changelog-worthy changes
Maintains consistent changelog format with Features, Fixes, and Changes sections
Updates both CHANGES.md and build.gradle.kts changeNotes simultaneously
Filters out internal changes and focuses on user-visible improvements
Handles version gaps and maintains historical context
author: JetBrains
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown instructions. It provides guidelines for maintaining the IdeaVim changelog and contains NO executable code, network calls, or file operations. All 110 static findings are false positives from the analyzer misinterpreting markdown code block syntax as shell commands and documentation URLs as network indicators.
valueStatement: Keeping changelogs updated is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates the process by analyzing commits, PRs, and releases to generate accurate, user-focused changelog entries following the IdeaVim format.
limitations: Specifically designed for IdeaVim project structure and conventions
Requires manual review before creating PR

useCases.title: Automate release documentation
useCases.description: Keep your project's changelog updated with minimal effort by automatically analyzing commits and PRs for user-facing changes.

useCases.title: Standardize release notes
useCases.description: Ensure consistent, professional release documentation that follows established patterns and includes proper references to issues and PRs.

useCases.title: Document community contributions
useCases.description: Properly acknowledge external contributors by automatically including their PRs in the changelog, even for internal changes.

promptTemplates.title: Update changelog for release
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the IdeaVim changelog for the upcoming 2.29.0 release. Check commits since the last documented version and include all user-facing changes.

promptTemplates.title: Add changes to unreleased
promptTemplates.prompt: Update the [To Be Released] section with recent commits from the master branch. Focus on user-visible features and bug fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Review PR for changelog
promptTemplates.scenario: Checking if a PR needs docs
promptTemplates.prompt: Review PR #1234 and determine if it needs a changelog entry. If so, prepare the appropriate entry following the changelog format.

promptTemplates.title: Generate changeNotes HTML
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert the latest [To Be Released] changelog entries to HTML format for the build.gradle.kts changeNotes section.

bestPractices: Always review generated changelog entries for accuracy and clarity before committing
Include specific examples of commands or features when describing fixes or new functionality
Link to relevant documentation or JetBrains blog posts for new IntelliJ integrations
antiPatterns: Don't include internal refactoring or code cleanup that doesn't affect users
Avoid vague descriptions like "Fixed bugs" without specifying what was fixed
Never skip the manual review step - automated changelog still needs human verification

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with non-IdeaVim projects?
faq.answer: No, it is specifically designed for IdeaVim's changelog format and conventions. Other projects would need adaptation.

faq.question: How does it handle the changelog gap between versions 2.9.0 and 2.28.0?
faq.answer: It automatically includes a note about the unmaintained period and continues documentation from 2.28.0 onwards.

faq.question: Can it automatically create and merge PRs?
faq.answer: No, it only prepares the changelog content. You must manually create and review the PR before merging.

faq.question: Is my code and commit history safe?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill only reads git history and generates text. It does not modify code or send data externally.

faq.question: What if the skill misses an important change?
faq.answer: Manual review is required. You can always add or modify entries before creating the PR.

faq.question: How does it differ from automatic git log generators?
faq.answer: It focuses on user-visible changes, filters internal updates, and follows specific formatting rules for IdeaVim's audience.
